412	        wait_for_server "$TP8_SIDECAR_PID" "http://127.0.0.1:${TP8_SIDECAR_PORT}/v1/models"
413	    fi
414	
415	    if [[ "$ENABLE_TP4_SIDECARS" == "1" ]]; then
416	        local sidecar_ports=("$TP4_SIDECAR_PORT_1" "$TP4_SIDECAR_PORT_2")
417	        local sidecar_cores=("48-51" "52-55")
418	        local index sidecar_port sidecar_cores_for_port sidecar_log
419	
420	        mkdir -p "$PLANNING_LOG_DIR"
421	        for index in "${!sidecar_ports[@]}"; do
422	            sidecar_port="${sidecar_ports[$index]}"
423	            sidecar_cores_for_port="${sidecar_cores[$index]}"
424	            sidecar_log="$PLANNING_LOG_DIR/tongyi-${sidecar_port}.log"
425	            echo "Starting experimental TP4 single-sequence sidecar: port=$sidecar_port cores=$sidecar_cores_for_port log=$sidecar_log"
426	            numactl --cpunodebind=1 --membind=1 \
427	                env \
428	                MODEL="$MODEL" \
429	                PORT="$sidecar_port" \
430	                TP_SIZE=4 \
431	                MAX_MODEL_LEN=128000 \
432	                MAX_NUM_SEQS=1 \
433	                NUM_GPU_BLOCKS_OVERRIDE=4000 \
434	                NEURON_RT_VISIBLE_CORES="$sidecar_cores_for_port" \
435	                NEURON_COMPILED_ARTIFACTS="$TP4_SIDECAR_ARTIFACT" \
436	                NEURON_REQUIRE_COMPILED_ARTIFACTS=1 \
437	                NEURON_SCHEDULER=custom \
438	                ENABLE_CHUNKED_PREFILL=0 \
439	                OVERRIDE_NEURON_CONFIG='{"enable_bucketing":true,"seq_len":128000,"max_context_length":128000,"context_encoding_buckets":[8192],"prefix_buckets":[122880],"token_generation_buckets":[16384,32768,65536,98304,128000],"save_sharded_checkpoint":true,"blockwise_matmul_config":{"use_shard_on_intermediate_dynamic_while":true}}' \
440	                NEURON_CACHE_TAG=tongyi-128k-prefix120-tp4-s1 \
441	                EXTRA_VLLM_ARGS="${TP4_SIDECAR_EXTRA_VLLM_ARGS:-}" \
442	                bash "$SCRIPT_DIR/serve_tongyi.bash" \
443	                >"$sidecar_log" 2>&1 &
444	            TP4_SIDECAR_PIDS+=("$!")
445	            wait_for_server "${TP4_SIDECAR_PIDS[$index]}" "http://127.0.0.1:${sidecar_port}/v1/models"
446	        done
447	    fi
448	}
449	
450	start_judge_server() {
451	    if [[ "$START_JUDGE_SERVER" != "1" ]]; then
452	        wait_for_url_list "$JUDGE_BASE_URLS"
453	        return
454	    fi
455	
456	    MODEL="$JUDGE_MODEL" \
457	    FIRST_PORT="$JUDGE_PORT" \
458	    JUDGE_REPLICAS="$JUDGE_REPLICAS" \
459	    TP_SIZE="$JUDGE_TP_SIZE" \
460	    MAX_MODEL_LEN="$JUDGE_MAX_MODEL_LEN" \
461	    MAX_NUM_SEQS="$JUDGE_MAX_NUM_SEQS" \
462	        bash "$SCRIPT_DIR/serve_qwen3_32b_judge_cluster.bash" &
463	    JUDGE_PID=$!
464	
465	    # JUDGE_PORT starts last and is the readiness barrier for the full cluster.
466	    wait_for_server "$JUDGE_PID" "http://127.0.0.1:${JUDGE_PORT}/v1/models"
467	}
468	
469	run_filter_all_tongyi() {
470	    local qrel_run="$1"
471	    local qrel_dir
472	    local qrel_label
473	    qrel_dir="$(qrel_dir_for_run "$qrel_run")"
474	    qrel_label="$(qrel_label_for_run "$qrel_run")"
475	    local output_dir="$RUN_ROOT/$qrel_label/$RUN_NAME"
476	    local cache_dir="$CACHE_ROOT/$qrel_label/$RUN_NAME"
477	    local max_query_args=()
478	    local endpoint_capacity_args=()
479	    local restart_args=()
480	    local prefix_guard_args=()
481	
482	    if [[ -n "$MAX_QUERIES" ]]; then
483	        max_query_args=(--max-queries "$MAX_QUERIES")
484	    fi
485	    if [[ -n "$PLANNING_ENDPOINT_CAPACITIES" ]]; then
486	        endpoint_capacity_args=(--planning-endpoint-capacities "$PLANNING_ENDPOINT_CAPACITIES")
487	    fi
488	    if [[ "$START_SERVER" == "1" ]]; then
489	        restart_args=(
490	            --planning-restart-dir "$PLANNING_RESTART_DIR"
491	            --planning-restart-timeout-seconds "$PLANNING_RESTART_TIMEOUT_SECONDS"
492	            --planning-query-retry-limit "$PLANNING_QUERY_RETRY_LIMIT"
493	        )
494	    fi
495	    if [[ -n "$PLANNING_MAX_UNCACHED_PREFILL_TOKENS" ]]; then
496	        prefix_guard_args=(--planning-max-uncached-prefill-tokens "$PLANNING_MAX_UNCACHED_PREFILL_TOKENS")
497	    fi
498	
499	    echo "Running filter_all Tongyi on $qrel_label without get_document on planning ports $PLANNING_PORTS"
500	    echo "Neuron embedding endpoint: http://127.0.0.1:$EMBEDDING_PORT"
501	    "$PYTHON_BIN" search_agent/tongyi_client.py \
502	        --model "$MODEL" \
503	        --query "$qrel_dir/queries.tsv" \
504	        --output-dir "$output_dir" \
505	        --cache-dir "$cache_dir" \
506	        --searcher-type faiss \
507	        --index-path "indexes/qwen3-embedding-8b/corpus.shard*.pkl" \
508	        --model-name "Qwen/Qwen3-Embedding-8B" \
509	        --normalize \
510	        --num-threads "$NUM_THREADS" \
511	        --embedding-base-url "http://127.0.0.1:$EMBEDDING_PORT" \
512	        --embedding-timeout 60 \
513	        --max-length 1024 \
514	        --planning-ports "$PLANNING_PORTS" \
515	        --planning-endpoint-capacity "$PLANNING_ENDPOINT_CAPACITY" \
516	        "${endpoint_capacity_args[@]}" \
517	        --planning-lease-scope "$PLANNING_LEASE_SCOPE" \
518	        --planning-affinity-timeout "$PLANNING_AFFINITY_TIMEOUT" \
519	        "${restart_args[@]}" \
520	        "${prefix_guard_args[@]}" \
521	        --snippet-max-tokens 512 \
522	        --k 5 \
523	        --port "$PORT" \
524	        --qrel-evidence-path "$qrel_dir/qrel_evidence.txt" \
525	        --qrel-golds-path "$qrel_dir/qrel_golds.txt" \
526	        --filter-all \
527	        "${max_query_args[@]}"
528	}
529	
530	# Previous full-qrels filter-non-positive runner.
531	# run_tongyi() {
532	#     local variant="$1"
533	#     local output_dir
534	#     local cache_dir
535	#     local extra_args=()
536	#
537	#     output_dir="$(variant_output_dir "$variant")"
538	#     cache_dir="$(variant_cache_dir "$variant")"
539	#
540	#     case "$variant" in
541	#         saturated | saturate)
542	#             extra_args=(--filter-non-positive --id-title-marker --filtered-empty-response saturated)
543	#             ;;
544	#         refer)
545	#             extra_args=(--filter-non-positive --id-title-marker --filtered-empty-response refer-to-context)
546	#             ;;
547	#         *)
548	#             echo "Unknown variant: $variant" >&2
549	#             return 1
550	#             ;;
551	#     esac
552	#
553	#     echo "Running $variant on full qrels with Neuron server at port $PORT"
554	#     "$PYTHON_BIN" search_agent/tongyi_client.py \
555	#         --model "$MODEL" \
556	#         --query "$QUERY_FILE" \
557	#         --output-dir "$output_dir" \
558	#         --cache-dir "$cache_dir" \
559	#         --searcher-type faiss \
560	#         --index-path "indexes/qwen3-embedding-8b/corpus.shard*.pkl" \
561	#         --model-name "Qwen/Qwen3-Embedding-8B" \
562	#         --normalize \
563	#         --num-threads "$NUM_THREADS" \
564	#         --snippet-max-tokens 512 \
565	#         --k 5 \
566	#         --get-document \
567	#         --port "$PORT" \
568	#         --qrel-evidence-path "$QREL_EVIDENCE_FILE" \
569	#         --qrel-golds-path "$QREL_GOLDS_FILE" \
570	#         "${extra_args[@]}"
571	# }
572	
573	start_tongyi_server
574	
575	for qrel_run in "${QREL_RUNS[@]}"; do
576	    run_filter_all_tongyi "$qrel_run"
577	done
578	
579	# for variant in $VARIANTS; do
580	#     run_tongyi "$variant"
581	# done
582	
583	cleanup_pid "$TONGYI_PID"
584	TONGYI_PID=""
585	cleanup_pid "$TP8_SIDECAR_PID"
586	TP8_SIDECAR_PID=""
587	cleanup_pids "${TP4_SIDECAR_PIDS[@]}"
588	TP4_SIDECAR_PIDS=()
589	summarize_planning_logs
590	
591	if [[ "$RUN_EVAL" == "1" ]]; then
592	    start_judge_server
593	
594	    for qrel_run in "${QREL_RUNS[@]}"; do
595	        qrel_dir="$(qrel_dir_for_run "$qrel_run")"
596	        qrel_label="$(qrel_label_for_run "$qrel_run")"
597	        "$PYTHON_BIN" scripts_evaluation/evaluate_run.py \
598	            --input_dir "$RUN_ROOT/$qrel_label/$RUN_NAME" \
599	            --qrel_evidence "$qrel_dir/qrel_evidence.txt" \
600	            --model "$JUDGE_MODEL" \
601	            --judge-backend openai-compatible \
602	            --judge-base-urls "$JUDGE_BASE_URLS" \
603	            --judge-num-threads "$JUDGE_NUM_THREADS" \

#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
REPO_ROOT="$(cd -- "$SCRIPT_DIR/../.." && pwd)"
cd -- "$REPO_ROOT"

MODEL="${MODEL:-Alibaba-NLP/Tongyi-DeepResearch-30B-A3B}"
PORT="${PORT:-6008}"
TONGYI_REPLICAS="${TONGYI_REPLICAS:-7}"
PLANNING_PORTS_WAS_SET="${PLANNING_PORTS:+1}"
PLANNING_PORTS="${PLANNING_PORTS:-$PORT-$((PORT + TONGYI_REPLICAS - 1))}"
PLANNING_ENDPOINT_CAPACITY="${PLANNING_ENDPOINT_CAPACITY:-1}"
PLANNING_ENDPOINT_CAPACITIES="${PLANNING_ENDPOINT_CAPACITIES:-}"
PLANNING_AFFINITY_TIMEOUT="${PLANNING_AFFINITY_TIMEOUT:-0.2}"
EMBEDDING_PORT="${EMBEDDING_PORT:-6030}"
TP_SIZE="${TP_SIZE:-8}"
MAX_MODEL_LEN="${MAX_MODEL_LEN:-128000}"
MAX_NUM_SEQS="${MAX_NUM_SEQS:-1}"
NEURON_SCHEDULER="${NEURON_SCHEDULER:-custom}"
ENABLE_TP8_SIDECAR="${ENABLE_TP8_SIDECAR:-0}"
TP8_SIDECAR_PORT="${TP8_SIDECAR_PORT:-$((PORT + TONGYI_REPLICAS))}"
TP8_SIDECAR_CORES="48-55"
TP8_SIDECAR_ARTIFACT="${TP8_SIDECAR_ARTIFACT:-$REPO_ROOT/local-models/neuron-compiled-artifacts/9a36595723d1059555571d3bf524fa88a507814a2efbe2917865b6350bb75912}"
ENABLE_TP4_SIDECARS="${ENABLE_TP4_SIDECARS:-0}"
TP4_SIDECAR_PORT_1="${TP4_SIDECAR_PORT_1:-$((PORT + TONGYI_REPLICAS))}"
TP4_SIDECAR_PORT_2="${TP4_SIDECAR_PORT_2:-$((PORT + TONGYI_REPLICAS + 1))}"
TP4_SIDECAR_ARTIFACT="${TP4_SIDECAR_ARTIFACT:-$REPO_ROOT/local-models/neuron-compiled-artifacts/27b98ea6f3cabed3fd9ed630d1a2996e28998618d11e43538edbd3d3e09c3586}"

planning_ports_include() {
    local target="$1" item start end
    local endpoints=()
    IFS=, read -r -a endpoints <<< "$PLANNING_PORTS"
    for item in "${endpoints[@]}"; do
        if [[ "$item" =~ ^([0-9]+)-([0-9]+)$ ]]; then
            start=$((10#${BASH_REMATCH[1]}))
            end=$((10#${BASH_REMATCH[2]}))
            if (( target >= start && target <= end )); then
                return 0
            fi
        elif [[ "$item" =~ ^[0-9]+$ ]] && (( target == 10#$item )); then
            return 0
        fi
    done
    return 1
}

# The current custom artifact cannot chunk a cold prompt whose uncached suffix
# exceeds 16k, so only two long trajectories may retain each endpoint's APC
# state. Native scheduling uses chunked prefill and may use any replica.
PLANNING_LEASE_SCOPE="${PLANNING_LEASE_SCOPE:-}"
if [[ -z "$PLANNING_LEASE_SCOPE" ]]; then
    if [[ "$NEURON_SCHEDULER" == "native" ]]; then
        PLANNING_LEASE_SCOPE="request"
    else
        PLANNING_LEASE_SCOPE="trajectory"
    fi
fi
case "$NEURON_SCHEDULER" in
    custom | native) ;;
    *)
        echo "NEURON_SCHEDULER must be custom or native" >&2
        exit 2
        ;;
esac
case "$PLANNING_LEASE_SCOPE" in
    trajectory | affinity | request) ;;
    *)
        echo "PLANNING_LEASE_SCOPE must be trajectory, affinity, or request" >&2
        exit 2
        ;;
esac
case "$ENABLE_TP8_SIDECAR" in
    0 | 1) ;;
    *)
        echo "ENABLE_TP8_SIDECAR must be 0 or 1" >&2
        exit 2
        ;;
esac
case "$ENABLE_TP4_SIDECARS" in
    0 | 1) ;;
    *)
        echo "ENABLE_TP4_SIDECARS must be 0 or 1" >&2
        exit 2
        ;;
esac
if [[ "$ENABLE_TP8_SIDECAR" == "1" && "$ENABLE_TP4_SIDECARS" == "1" ]]; then
    echo "Choose either ENABLE_TP8_SIDECAR or ENABLE_TP4_SIDECARS, not both." >&2
    exit 2
fi
# TP=8, single-sequence artifact produced by compile_tongyi_tp8_s1.bash.
DEFAULT_TONGYI_ARTIFACT="$REPO_ROOT/local-models/neuron-compiled-artifacts/9a36595723d1059555571d3bf524fa88a507814a2efbe2917865b6350bb75912"
export NEURON_CACHE_TAG="${NEURON_CACHE_TAG:-tongyi-128k-prefix112-tp8-s1}"
export NEURON_COMPILED_ARTIFACTS="${NEURON_COMPILED_ARTIFACTS:-$DEFAULT_TONGYI_ARTIFACT}"
if [[ "$NEURON_SCHEDULER" == "native" && "$NEURON_COMPILED_ARTIFACTS" == "$DEFAULT_TONGYI_ARTIFACT" ]]; then
    echo "NEURON_SCHEDULER=native requires a separately compiled native artifact; set NEURON_COMPILED_ARTIFACTS." >&2
    exit 2
fi
if [[ "$NEURON_SCHEDULER" == "custom" \
    && ( "$PLANNING_LEASE_SCOPE" == "request" || "$PLANNING_LEASE_SCOPE" == "affinity" ) ]]; then
    echo "custom scheduler requires trajectory leases: released KV blocks are evictable, so request or affinity leases can lose a long APC history and kill EngineCore. Use NEURON_SCHEDULER=native for non-trajectory leases." >&2
    exit 2
fi
if [[ ! -f "$NEURON_COMPILED_ARTIFACTS/model.pt" ]]; then
    echo "No complete Tongyi artifact at: $NEURON_COMPILED_ARTIFACTS" >&2
    echo "Compile the matching scheduler profile before starting runtime replicas." >&2
    exit 2
fi
if [[ "$ENABLE_TP8_SIDECAR" == "1" ]]; then
    if [[ "$NEURON_SCHEDULER" != "custom" || "$PLANNING_LEASE_SCOPE" != "trajectory" ]]; then
        echo "TP8 sidecar only supports custom scheduling with trajectory leases; compile a native TP8 artifact before mixing it with request leases." >&2
        exit 2
    fi
    if [[ "$TP_SIZE" != "16" || "$TONGYI_REPLICAS" != "3" || "$MAX_NUM_SEQS" != "2" ]]; then
        echo "TP8 sidecar requires the 3xTP16, MAX_NUM_SEQS=2 planning topology." >&2
        exit 2
    fi
    if [[ ! -f "$TP8_SIDECAR_ARTIFACT/model.pt" ]]; then
        echo "No complete TP8 sidecar artifact at: $TP8_SIDECAR_ARTIFACT" >&2
        exit 2
    fi
    if [[ -z "$PLANNING_PORTS_WAS_SET" ]]; then
        PLANNING_PORTS="$PLANNING_PORTS,$TP8_SIDECAR_PORT"
    elif ! planning_ports_include "$TP8_SIDECAR_PORT"; then
        echo "PLANNING_PORTS must include TP8_SIDECAR_PORT=$TP8_SIDECAR_PORT when ENABLE_TP8_SIDECAR=1." >&2
        exit 2
    fi
    if [[ -z "$PLANNING_ENDPOINT_CAPACITIES" ]]; then
        PLANNING_ENDPOINT_CAPACITIES="${PORT}:2,$((PORT + 1)):2,$((PORT + 2)):2,${TP8_SIDECAR_PORT}:1"
    fi
fi
if [[ "$ENABLE_TP4_SIDECARS" == "1" ]]; then
    if [[ "$NEURON_SCHEDULER" != "custom" || "$PLANNING_LEASE_SCOPE" != "trajectory" ]]; then
        echo "TP4 sidecars only support custom scheduling with trajectory leases; compile native TP4 artifacts before mixing them with request leases." >&2
        exit 2
    fi
    if [[ "$TP_SIZE" != "16" || "$TONGYI_REPLICAS" != "3" || "$MAX_NUM_SEQS" != "2" ]]; then
        echo "TP4 sidecars require the 3xTP16, MAX_NUM_SEQS=2 planning topology." >&2
        exit 2
    fi
    if [[ ! "$TP4_SIDECAR_PORT_1" =~ ^[1-9][0-9]*$ ]] \
        || [[ ! "$TP4_SIDECAR_PORT_2" =~ ^[1-9][0-9]*$ ]] \
        || (( TP4_SIDECAR_PORT_1 > 65535 || TP4_SIDECAR_PORT_2 > 65535 )) \
        || [[ "$TP4_SIDECAR_PORT_1" == "$TP4_SIDECAR_PORT_2" ]]; then
        echo "TP4_SIDECAR_PORT_1 and TP4_SIDECAR_PORT_2 must be distinct ports in 1..65535." >&2
        exit 2
    fi
    if [[ ! -f "$TP4_SIDECAR_ARTIFACT/model.pt" ]]; then
        echo "No complete TP4 sidecar artifact at: $TP4_SIDECAR_ARTIFACT" >&2
        echo "Smoke the TP4 artifact before using this experimental topology." >&2
        exit 2
    fi
    if [[ -z "$PLANNING_PORTS_WAS_SET" ]]; then
        PLANNING_PORTS="$PLANNING_PORTS,$TP4_SIDECAR_PORT_1,$TP4_SIDECAR_PORT_2"
    elif ! planning_ports_include "$TP4_SIDECAR_PORT_1" \
        || ! planning_ports_include "$TP4_SIDECAR_PORT_2"; then
        echo "PLANNING_PORTS must include both TP4 sidecar ports when ENABLE_TP4_SIDECARS=1." >&2
        exit 2
    fi
    if [[ -z "$PLANNING_ENDPOINT_CAPACITIES" ]]; then
        PLANNING_ENDPOINT_CAPACITIES="${PORT}:2,$((PORT + 1)):2,$((PORT + 2)):2,${TP4_SIDECAR_PORT_1}:1,${TP4_SIDECAR_PORT_2}:1"
    fi
fi
PYTHON_BIN="${PYTHON_BIN:-$REPO_ROOT/.venv/bin/python}"
NUM_THREADS="${NUM_THREADS:-$((TONGYI_REPLICAS * 2))}"
RUN_EVAL="${RUN_EVAL:-1}"
EVAL_BATCH_SIZE="${EVAL_BATCH_SIZE:-32}"
MAX_QUERIES="${MAX_QUERIES:-}"
RUN_NAME="${RUN_NAME:-tongyi_filter_all_no_get}"
QREL_RUNS=(8 4 full)
# Previous full-qrels filter-non-positive variants. Keep as reference; this
# script now runs filter_all on 1/8, then 1/4, then the full qrels.
# VARIANTS="${VARIANTS:-saturate refer}"

START_SERVER="${START_SERVER:-1}"
START_JUDGE_SERVER="${START_JUDGE_SERVER:-1}"
JUDGE_MODEL="${JUDGE_MODEL:-Qwen/Qwen3-32B}"
JUDGE_PORT="${JUDGE_PORT:-8032}"
JUDGE_REPLICAS="${JUDGE_REPLICAS:-16}"
JUDGE_TP_SIZE="${JUDGE_TP_SIZE:-4}"
JUDGE_MAX_MODEL_LEN="${JUDGE_MAX_MODEL_LEN:-8192}"
JUDGE_MAX_NUM_SEQS="${JUDGE_MAX_NUM_SEQS:-1}"
JUDGE_NUM_THREADS="${JUDGE_NUM_THREADS:-16}"
JUDGE_BASE_URLS="${JUDGE_BASE_URLS:-}"

RUN_ROOT="${RUN_ROOT:-runs/qwen3-8b}"
CACHE_ROOT="${CACHE_ROOT:-cache/qwen3-8b}"
PLANNING_LOG_DIR="${PLANNING_LOG_DIR:-$REPO_ROOT/logs/neuron-servers/$(date -u +%Y%m%dT%H%M%SZ)}"
PLANNING_RESTART_DIR="${PLANNING_RESTART_DIR:-$PLANNING_LOG_DIR/restarts}"
PLANNING_RESTART_TIMEOUT_SECONDS="${PLANNING_RESTART_TIMEOUT_SECONDS:-600}"
PLANNING_QUERY_RETRY_LIMIT="${PLANNING_QUERY_RETRY_LIMIT:-2}"
PLANNING_MAX_UNCACHED_PREFILL_TOKENS="${PLANNING_MAX_UNCACHED_PREFILL_TOKENS:-}"
if [[ -z "$PLANNING_MAX_UNCACHED_PREFILL_TOKENS" && "$NEURON_SCHEDULER" == "custom" ]]; then
    PLANNING_MAX_UNCACHED_PREFILL_TOKENS=16384
fi
SUMMARIZE_NEURON_LOGS="${SUMMARIZE_NEURON_LOGS:-1}"

TONGYI_PID=""
TP8_SIDECAR_PID=""
TP4_SIDECAR_PIDS=()
JUDGE_PID=""
PLANNING_LOGS_SUMMARIZED=0

cleanup_pid() {
    local pid="$1"
    if [[ -n "$pid" ]] && kill -0 "$pid" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        kill "$pid" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
        wait "$pid" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
    fi
}

cleanup_pids() {
    local pid
    for pid in "$@"; do
        cleanup_pid "$pid"
    done
}

summarize_planning_logs() {
    local log logs=() summary_path temporary_path

    [[ "$SUMMARIZE_NEURON_LOGS" == "1" ]] || return 0
    [[ "$START_SERVER" == "1" ]] || return 0
    [[ "$PLANNING_LOGS_SUMMARIZED" == "0" ]] || return 0

    for log in "$PLANNING_LOG_DIR"/tongyi-*.log; do
        [[ -f "$log" ]] && logs+=("$log")
    done
    (( ${#logs[@]} > 0 )) || return 0

    summary_path="$PLANNING_LOG_DIR/planning_core_time.json"
    temporary_path="$summary_path.tmp.$$"
    if "$PYTHON_BIN" scripts/neuron_summarize_vllm_logs.py \
        --year "$(date -u +%Y)" \
        --json \
        "${logs[@]}" >"$temporary_path"; then
        mv -- "$temporary_path" "$summary_path"
        echo "Planning slot-duty and vacancy summary: $summary_path"
        PLANNING_LOGS_SUMMARIZED=1
    else
        rm -f -- "$temporary_path"
        echo "WARNING: could not summarize planning logs under $PLANNING_LOG_DIR" >&2
    fi
}

cleanup() {
    cleanup_pid "$TONGYI_PID"
    cleanup_pid "$TP8_SIDECAR_PID"
    cleanup_pids "${TP4_SIDECAR_PIDS[@]}"
    summarize_planning_logs
    cleanup_pid "$JUDGE_PID"
}
trap cleanup EXIT
trap 'exit 130' INT TERM

qrel_dir_for_run() {
    case "$1" in
        8 | 4) printf "topics-qrels/topics-qrels-%s\n" "$1" ;;
        full) printf "%s\n" "topics-qrels" ;;
        *) echo "Unknown qrel run: $1" >&2; return 1 ;;
    esac
}

qrel_label_for_run() {
    case "$1" in
        8 | 4) printf "%sqrel\n" "$1" ;;
        full) printf "%s\n" "full" ;;
        *) echo "Unknown qrel run: $1" >&2; return 1 ;;
    esac
}

for qrel_run in "${QREL_RUNS[@]}"; do
    qrel_dir="$(qrel_dir_for_run "$qrel_run")"
    for required_file in queries.tsv qrel_evidence.txt qrel_golds.txt; do
        if [[ ! -f "$qrel_dir/$required_file" ]]; then
            echo "Missing required file: $qrel_dir/$required_file" >&2
            exit 1
        fi
    done
done

wait_for_server() {
    local pid="$1"
    local url="$2"

    until curl -fsS "$url" >/dev/null; do
        if [[ -n "$pid" ]] && ! kill -0 "$pid" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
            wait "$pid"
            exit 1
        fi
        sleep 10
    done
}

wait_for_planning_endpoints() {
    local item start end port
    local endpoints=()
    IFS=, read -r -a endpoints <<< "$PLANNING_PORTS"
    for item in "${endpoints[@]}"; do
        if [[ "$item" =~ ^([0-9]+)-([0-9]+)$ ]]; then
            start=$((10#${BASH_REMATCH[1]}))
            end=$((10#${BASH_REMATCH[2]}))
            if (( start < 1 || end > 65535 || end < start )); then
                echo "Invalid planning port range: $item" >&2
                exit 2
            fi
            for ((port = start; port <= end; port++)); do
                wait_for_server "" "http://127.0.0.1:${port}/v1/models"
            done
        elif [[ "$item" =~ ^[0-9]+$ ]]; then
            port=$((10#$item))
            if (( port < 1 || port > 65535 )); then
                echo "Invalid planning port: $item" >&2
                exit 2
            fi
            wait_for_server "" "http://127.0.0.1:${port}/v1/models"
        else
            echo "Invalid planning port entry: $item" >&2
            exit 2
        fi
    done
}

if [[ -z "$JUDGE_BASE_URLS" ]]; then
    judge_urls=()
    for ((replica = 0; replica < JUDGE_REPLICAS; replica++)); do
        judge_urls+=("http://127.0.0.1:$((JUDGE_PORT + replica))/v1")
    done
    JUDGE_BASE_URLS="$(IFS=,; echo "${judge_urls[*]}")"
fi

wait_for_url_list() {
    local raw_urls="$1"
    local urls=()
    local url
    IFS=, read -r -a urls <<< "$raw_urls"
    for url in "${urls[@]}"; do
        wait_for_server "" "${url%/}/models"
    done
}

start_tongyi_server() {
    if [[ "$START_SERVER" != "1" ]]; then
        wait_for_planning_endpoints
        wait_for_server "" "http://127.0.0.1:${EMBEDDING_PORT}/health"
        return
    fi

    EXTRA_VLLM_ARGS="--no-async-scheduling${EXTRA_VLLM_ARGS:+ $EXTRA_VLLM_ARGS}" \
    MODEL="$MODEL" \
    FIRST_PORT="$PORT" \
    TONGYI_REPLICAS="$TONGYI_REPLICAS" \
    EMBEDDING_PORT="$EMBEDDING_PORT" \
    TP_SIZE="$TP_SIZE" \
    MAX_MODEL_LEN="$MAX_MODEL_LEN" \
    MAX_NUM_SEQS="$MAX_NUM_SEQS" \
    NEURON_SCHEDULER="$NEURON_SCHEDULER" \
    NEURON_COMPILED_ARTIFACTS="$NEURON_COMPILED_ARTIFACTS" \
    LOG_DIR="$PLANNING_LOG_DIR" \
    PLANNING_RESTART_DIR="$PLANNING_RESTART_DIR" \
        bash "$SCRIPT_DIR/serve_tongyi_cluster.bash" &
    TONGYI_PID=$!

    for ((replica = 0; replica < TONGYI_REPLICAS; replica++)); do
        wait_for_server \
            "$TONGYI_PID" \
            "http://127.0.0.1:$((PORT + replica))/v1/models"
    done
    wait_for_server "$TONGYI_PID" "http://127.0.0.1:${EMBEDDING_PORT}/health"

    if [[ "$ENABLE_TP8_SIDECAR" == "1" ]]; then
        local sidecar_log_dir="${TP8_SIDECAR_LOG_DIR:-$PLANNING_LOG_DIR}"
        local sidecar_log="$sidecar_log_dir/tongyi-${TP8_SIDECAR_PORT}.log"
        mkdir -p "$sidecar_log_dir"
        echo "Starting TP8 single-sequence sidecar: port=$TP8_SIDECAR_PORT cores=$TP8_SIDECAR_CORES log=$sidecar_log"
        numactl --cpunodebind=1 --membind=1 \
            env \
            MODEL="$MODEL" \
            PORT="$TP8_SIDECAR_PORT" \
            TP_SIZE=8 \
            MAX_MODEL_LEN=128000 \
            MAX_NUM_SEQS=1 \
            NUM_GPU_BLOCKS_OVERRIDE=4000 \
            NEURON_RT_VISIBLE_CORES="$TP8_SIDECAR_CORES" \
            NEURON_COMPILED_ARTIFACTS="$TP8_SIDECAR_ARTIFACT" \
            NEURON_REQUIRE_COMPILED_ARTIFACTS=1 \
            NEURON_SCHEDULER=custom \
            ENABLE_CHUNKED_PREFILL=0 \
            OVERRIDE_NEURON_CONFIG="" \
            NEURON_CACHE_TAG=tongyi-128k-prefix112-tp8-s1 \
            EXTRA_VLLM_ARGS="${TP8_SIDECAR_EXTRA_VLLM_ARGS:-}" \
            bash "$SCRIPT_DIR/serve_tongyi_tp8_s1.bash" \
            >"$sidecar_log" 2>&1 &
        TP8_SIDECAR_PID=$!
        wait_for_server "$TP8_SIDECAR_PID" "http://127.0.0.1:${TP8_SIDECAR_PORT}/v1/models"
    fi

    if [[ "$ENABLE_TP4_SIDECARS" == "1" ]]; then
        local sidecar_ports=("$TP4_SIDECAR_PORT_1" "$TP4_SIDECAR_PORT_2")
        local sidecar_cores=("48-51" "52-55")
        local index sidecar_port sidecar_cores_for_port sidecar_log

        mkdir -p "$PLANNING_LOG_DIR"
        for index in "${!sidecar_ports[@]}"; do
            sidecar_port="${sidecar_ports[$index]}"
            sidecar_cores_for_port="${sidecar_cores[$index]}"
            sidecar_log="$PLANNING_LOG_DIR/tongyi-${sidecar_port}.log"
            echo "Starting experimental TP4 single-sequence sidecar: port=$sidecar_port cores=$sidecar_cores_for_port log=$sidecar_log"
            numactl --cpunodebind=1 --membind=1 \
                env \
                MODEL="$MODEL" \
                PORT="$sidecar_port" \
                TP_SIZE=4 \
                MAX_MODEL_LEN=128000 \
                MAX_NUM_SEQS=1 \
                NUM_GPU_BLOCKS_OVERRIDE=4000 \
                NEURON_RT_VISIBLE_CORES="$sidecar_cores_for_port" \
                NEURON_COMPILED_ARTIFACTS="$TP4_SIDECAR_ARTIFACT" \
                NEURON_REQUIRE_COMPILED_ARTIFACTS=1 \
                NEURON_SCHEDULER=custom \
                ENABLE_CHUNKED_PREFILL=0 \
                OVERRIDE_NEURON_CONFIG='{"enable_bucketing":true,"seq_len":128000,"max_context_length":128000,"context_encoding_buckets":[8192],"prefix_buckets":[122880],"token_generation_buckets":[16384,32768,65536,98304,128000],"save_sharded_checkpoint":true,"blockwise_matmul_config":{"use_shard_on_intermediate_dynamic_while":true}}' \
                NEURON_CACHE_TAG=tongyi-128k-prefix120-tp4-s1 \
                EXTRA_VLLM_ARGS="${TP4_SIDECAR_EXTRA_VLLM_ARGS:-}" \
                bash "$SCRIPT_DIR/serve_tongyi.bash" \
                >"$sidecar_log" 2>&1 &
            TP4_SIDECAR_PIDS+=("$!")
            wait_for_server "${TP4_SIDECAR_PIDS[$index]}" "http://127.0.0.1:${sidecar_port}/v1/models"
        done
    fi
}

start_judge_server() {
    if [[ "$START_JUDGE_SERVER" != "1" ]]; then
        wait_for_url_list "$JUDGE_BASE_URLS"
        return
    fi

    MODEL="$JUDGE_MODEL" \
    FIRST_PORT="$JUDGE_PORT" \
    JUDGE_REPLICAS="$JUDGE_REPLICAS" \
    TP_SIZE="$JUDGE_TP_SIZE" \
    MAX_MODEL_LEN="$JUDGE_MAX_MODEL_LEN" \
    MAX_NUM_SEQS="$JUDGE_MAX_NUM_SEQS" \
        bash "$SCRIPT_DIR/serve_qwen3_32b_judge_cluster.bash" &
    JUDGE_PID=$!

    # JUDGE_PORT starts last and is the readiness barrier for the full cluster.
    wait_for_server "$JUDGE_PID" "http://127.0.0.1:${JUDGE_PORT}/v1/models"
}

run_filter_all_tongyi() {
    local qrel_run="$1"
    local qrel_dir
    local qrel_label
    qrel_dir="$(qrel_dir_for_run "$qrel_run")"
    qrel_label="$(qrel_label_for_run "$qrel_run")"
    local output_dir="$RUN_ROOT/$qrel_label/$RUN_NAME"
    local cache_dir="$CACHE_ROOT/$qrel_label/$RUN_NAME"
    local max_query_args=()
    local endpoint_capacity_args=()
    local restart_args=()
    local prefix_guard_args=()

    if [[ -n "$MAX_QUERIES" ]]; then
        max_query_args=(--max-queries "$MAX_QUERIES")
    fi
    if [[ -n "$PLANNING_ENDPOINT_CAPACITIES" ]]; then
        endpoint_capacity_args=(--planning-endpoint-capacities "$PLANNING_ENDPOINT_CAPACITIES")
    fi
    if [[ "$START_SERVER" == "1" ]]; then
        restart_args=(
            --planning-restart-dir "$PLANNING_RESTART_DIR"
            --planning-restart-timeout-seconds "$PLANNING_RESTART_TIMEOUT_SECONDS"
            --planning-query-retry-limit "$PLANNING_QUERY_RETRY_LIMIT"
        )
    fi
    if [[ -n "$PLANNING_MAX_UNCACHED_PREFILL_TOKENS" ]]; then
        prefix_guard_args=(--planning-max-uncached-prefill-tokens "$PLANNING_MAX_UNCACHED_PREFILL_TOKENS")
    fi

    echo "Running filter_all Tongyi on $qrel_label without get_document on planning ports $PLANNING_PORTS"
    echo "Neuron embedding endpoint: http://127.0.0.1:$EMBEDDING_PORT"
    "$PYTHON_BIN" search_agent/tongyi_client.py \
        --model "$MODEL" \
        --query "$qrel_dir/queries.tsv" \
        --output-dir "$output_dir" \
        --cache-dir "$cache_dir" \
        --searcher-type faiss \
        --index-path "indexes/qwen3-embedding-8b/corpus.shard*.pkl" \
        --model-name "Qwen/Qwen3-Embedding-8B" \
        --normalize \
        --num-threads "$NUM_THREADS" \
        --embedding-base-url "http://127.0.0.1:$EMBEDDING_PORT" \
        --embedding-timeout 60 \
        --max-length 1024 \
        --planning-ports "$PLANNING_PORTS" \
        --planning-endpoint-capacity "$PLANNING_ENDPOINT_CAPACITY" \
        "${endpoint_capacity_args[@]}" \
        --planning-lease-scope "$PLANNING_LEASE_SCOPE" \
        --planning-affinity-timeout "$PLANNING_AFFINITY_TIMEOUT" \
        "${restart_args[@]}" \
        "${prefix_guard_args[@]}" \
        --snippet-max-tokens 512 \
        --k 5 \
        --port "$PORT" \
        --qrel-evidence-path "$qrel_dir/qrel_evidence.txt" \
        --qrel-golds-path "$qrel_dir/qrel_golds.txt" \
        --filter-all \
        "${max_query_args[@]}"
}

start_tongyi_server

for qrel_run in "${QREL_RUNS[@]}"; do
    run_filter_all_tongyi "$qrel_run"
done

# for variant in $VARIANTS; do
#     run_tongyi "$variant"

cleanup_pid "$TONGYI_PID"
TONGYI_PID=""
cleanup_pid "$TP8_SIDECAR_PID"
TP8_SIDECAR_PID=""
cleanup_pids "${TP4_SIDECAR_PIDS[@]}"
TP4_SIDECAR_PIDS=()
summarize_planning_logs

if [[ "$RUN_EVAL" == "1" ]]; then
    start_judge_server

    for qrel_run in "${QREL_RUNS[@]}"; do

1	"""HTTP microbatch service for a statically compiled Qwen3 Neuron embedder."""
2	
3	import argparse
4	import asyncio
5	import time
6	from concurrent.futures import ThreadPoolExecutor
7	from contextlib import asynccontextmanager
8	from dataclasses import dataclass
9	from typing import Callable
10	
11	import torch
12	import torch.nn.functional as F
13	from fastapi import FastAPI, HTTPException
14	from pydantic import BaseModel, Field
15	
16	
17	class EmbeddingInputError(ValueError):
18	    pass
19	
20	
21	@dataclass
22	class _WorkItem:
23	    text: str
24	    future: asyncio.Future
25	    enqueued_at: float
26	
27	
28	class StaticBatchEmbedder:
29	    def __init__(
30	        self,
31	        infer_batch: Callable[[list[str]], list[list[float]]],
32	        batch_size: int = 2,
33	        wait_ms: float = 5.0,
34	    ) -> None:
35	        if batch_size != 2:
36	            raise ValueError("the compiled embedding profile requires batch_size=2")
37	        self.infer_batch = infer_batch
38	        self.batch_size = batch_size
39	        self.wait_seconds = wait_ms / 1000
40	        self.queue: asyncio.Queue[_WorkItem] = asyncio.Queue()
41	        self._runner: asyncio.Task | None = None
42	        self._executor: ThreadPoolExecutor | None = None
43	        self._started_at = time.perf_counter()
44	        self._submitted_requests = 0
45	        self._resolved_requests = 0
46	        self._batches = 0
47	        self._duplicated_slots = 0
48	        self._queue_wait_seconds = 0.0
49	        self._inference_busy_seconds = 0.0
50	
51	    async def start(self) -> None:
52	        if self._runner is None:
53	            self._executor = ThreadPoolExecutor(
54	                max_workers=1,
55	                thread_name_prefix="neuron-embed",
56	            )
57	            self._runner = asyncio.create_task(self._run())
58	
59	    async def stop(self) -> None:
60	        if self._runner is not None:
61	            self._runner.cancel()
62	            try:
63	                await self._runner
64	            except asyncio.CancelledError:
65	                pass
66	            self._runner = None
67	        if self._executor is not None:
68	            # The runner is cancelled only between batches or after the current
69	            # synchronous inference has completed. Shut down its dedicated
70	            # worker explicitly so FastAPI/uvicorn can exit without retaining
71	            # an asyncio default-executor thread.
72	            self._executor.shutdown(wait=True, cancel_futures=True)
73	            self._executor = None
74	
75	    async def embed(self, texts: list[str]) -> list[list[float]]:
76	        if not texts:
77	            raise EmbeddingInputError("texts must not be empty")
78	        loop = asyncio.get_running_loop()
79	        futures = []
80	        enqueued_at = time.perf_counter()
81	        for text in texts:
82	            future = loop.create_future()
83	            futures.append(future)
84	            await self.queue.put(
85	                _WorkItem(text=text, future=future, enqueued_at=enqueued_at)
86	            )
87	        self._submitted_requests += len(futures)
88	        return await asyncio.gather(*futures)
89	
90	    def metrics(self) -> dict[str, float | int]:
91	        """Return process-lifetime batcher timings, not device MFU."""
92	        uptime_seconds = time.perf_counter() - self._started_at
93	        busy_duty_percent = (
94	            100 * self._inference_busy_seconds / uptime_seconds
95	            if uptime_seconds > 0
96	            else 0.0
97	        )
98	        return {
99	            "uptime_seconds": uptime_seconds,
100	            "submitted_requests": self._submitted_requests,
101	            "resolved_requests": self._resolved_requests,
102	            "batches": self._batches,
103	            "duplicated_slots": self._duplicated_slots,
104	            "queue_wait_seconds": self._queue_wait_seconds,
105	            "inference_busy_seconds": self._inference_busy_seconds,
106	            "batcher_busy_duty_percent": busy_duty_percent,
107	            "queue_depth": self.queue.qsize(),
108	        }
109	
110	    async def _run(self) -> None:
111	        while True:
112	            first = await self.queue.get()
113	            items = [first]
114	            deadline = asyncio.get_running_loop().time() + self.wait_seconds
115	            while len(items) < self.batch_size:
116	                remaining = deadline - asyncio.get_running_loop().time()
117	                if remaining <= 0:
118	                    break
119	                try:
120	                    items.append(await asyncio.wait_for(self.queue.get(), remaining))
121	                except asyncio.TimeoutError:
122	                    break
123	
124	            batch_texts = [item.text for item in items]
125	            if len(batch_texts) == 1:
126	                batch_texts.append(batch_texts[0])
127	                self._duplicated_slots += 1
128	
129	            inference_start = time.perf_counter()
130	            self._queue_wait_seconds += sum(
131	                inference_start - item.enqueued_at for item in items
132	            )
133	            try:
134	                executor = self._executor
135	                if executor is None:
136	                    raise RuntimeError("embedding batcher stopped while a batch was running")
137	                loop = asyncio.get_running_loop()
138	                embeddings = await loop.run_in_executor(
139	                    executor,
140	                    self.infer_batch,
141	                    batch_texts,
142	                )
143	                if len(embeddings) != self.batch_size:
144	                    raise RuntimeError(
145	                        f"embedding model returned {len(embeddings)} rows; "
146	                        f"expected {self.batch_size}"
147	                    )
148	                for item, embedding in zip(items, embeddings):
149	                        if not item.future.cancelled():
150	                            item.future.set_result(embedding)
151	                self._resolved_requests += len(items)
152	            except Exception as exc:
153	                for item in items:
154	                        if not item.future.cancelled():
155	                            item.future.set_exception(exc)
156	                self._resolved_requests += len(items)
157	            finally:
158	                self._inference_busy_seconds += time.perf_counter() - inference_start
159	                self._batches += 1
160	                for _ in items:
161	                    self.queue.task_done()
162	
163	
164	class NeuronEmbeddingModel:
165	    def __init__(
166	        self,
167	        compiled_model_path: str,
168	        tokenizer_path: str,
169	        sequence_length: int = 1024,
170	        embedding_dimension: int = 4096,
171	    ) -> None:
172	        from optimum.neuron import NeuronModelForEmbedding
173	        from transformers import AutoTokenizer
174	
175	        self.sequence_length = sequence_length
176	        self.embedding_dimension = embedding_dimension
177	        self.tokenizer = AutoTokenizer.from_pretrained(
178	            tokenizer_path,
179	            padding_side="right",
180	        )
181	        self.model = NeuronModelForEmbedding.from_pretrained(compiled_model_path)
182	
183	    def _validate_lengths(self, texts: list[str]) -> None:
184	        tokenized = self.tokenizer(
185	            texts,
186	            padding=False,
187	            truncation=False,
188	            add_special_tokens=True,
189	        )
190	        lengths = [len(input_ids) for input_ids in tokenized["input_ids"]]
191	        too_long = [length for length in lengths if length > self.sequence_length]
192	        if too_long:
193	            raise EmbeddingInputError(
194	                f"input has {max(too_long)} tokens; compiled limit is "
195	                f"{self.sequence_length}"
196	            )
197	
198	    def embed_batch(self, texts: list[str]) -> list[list[float]]:
199	        if len(texts) != 2:
200	            raise ValueError("compiled embedding model requires exactly two inputs")
201	        self._validate_lengths(texts)
202	        tokens = self.tokenizer(
203	            texts,
204	            padding="max_length",
205	            truncation=False,
206	            max_length=self.sequence_length,
207	            return_tensors="pt",
208	        )
209	        outputs = self.model(**tokens)
210	        if hasattr(outputs, "last_hidden_state"):
211	            hidden_states = outputs.last_hidden_state
212	        elif isinstance(outputs, (tuple, list)):
213	            hidden_states = outputs[0]
214	        else:
215	            hidden_states = outputs
216	
217	        if hidden_states.shape != (2, 1, self.embedding_dimension):
218	            raise RuntimeError(
219	                f"embedding model output shape is {tuple(hidden_states.shape)}; "
220	                f"expected (2, 1, {self.embedding_dimension})"
221	            )
222	        embeddings = hidden_states[:, 0, :]
223	        embeddings = F.normalize(embeddings.float(), p=2, dim=1)
224	        if embeddings.shape != (2, self.embedding_dimension):
225	            raise RuntimeError(
226	                f"embedding shape is {tuple(embeddings.shape)}; expected "
227	                f"(2, {self.embedding_dimension})"
228	            )
229	        return embeddings.cpu().tolist()
230	
231	
232	class EmbedRequest(BaseModel):
233	    texts: list[str] = Field(min_length=1, max_length=64)
234	
235	
236	class EmbedResponse(BaseModel):
237	    embeddings: list[list[float]]
238	    dimension: int
239	
240	
241	def create_app(model: NeuronEmbeddingModel, wait_ms: float = 5.0) -> FastAPI:
242	    batcher = StaticBatchEmbedder(model.embed_batch, wait_ms=wait_ms)
243	
244	    @asynccontextmanager
245	    async def lifespan(_: FastAPI):
246	        await batcher.start()
247	        try:
248	            yield
249	        finally:
250	            await batcher.stop()
251	
252	    app = FastAPI(lifespan=lifespan)
253	
254	    @app.get("/health")
255	    async def health():
256	        return {
257	            "status": "ok",
258	            "batch_size": batcher.batch_size,
259	            "sequence_length": model.sequence_length,
260	            "dimension": model.embedding_dimension,
261	        }
262	
263	    @app.get("/metrics")
264	    async def metrics():
265	        return {
266	            "batch_size": batcher.batch_size,
267	            "sequence_length": model.sequence_length,
268	            "dimension": model.embedding_dimension,
269	            **batcher.metrics(),
270	            "note": "Batcher busy duty is wall-clock inference occupancy, not Neuron MFU.",
271	        }
272	
273	    @app.post("/embed", response_model=EmbedResponse)
274	    async def embed(request: EmbedRequest):
275	        try:
276	            embeddings = await batcher.embed(request.texts)
277	        except EmbeddingInputError as exc:
278	            raise HTTPException(status_code=422, detail=str(exc)) from exc
279	        except Exception as exc:
280	            raise HTTPException(status_code=500, detail=str(exc)) from exc

file.content:
"""HTTP microbatch service for a statically compiled Qwen3 Neuron embedder."""

import argparse
import asyncio
import time
from concurrent.futures import ThreadPoolExecutor
from contextlib import asynccontextmanager
from dataclasses import dataclass
from typing import Callable

import torch
import torch.nn.functional as F
from fastapi import FastAPI, HTTPException
from pydantic import BaseModel, Field


class EmbeddingInputError(ValueError):
    pass


@dataclass
class _WorkItem:
    text: str
    future: asyncio.Future
    enqueued_at: float


class StaticBatchEmbedder:
    def __init__(
        self,
        infer_batch: Callable[[list[str]], list[list[float]]],
        batch_size: int = 2,
        wait_ms: float = 5.0,
    ) -> None:
        if batch_size != 2:
            raise ValueError("the compiled embedding profile requires batch_size=2")
        self.infer_batch = infer_batch
        self.batch_size = batch_size
        self.wait_seconds = wait_ms / 1000
        self.queue: asyncio.Queue[_WorkItem] = asyncio.Queue()
        self._runner: asyncio.Task | None = None
        self._executor: ThreadPoolExecutor | None = None
        self._started_at = time.perf_counter()
        self._submitted_requests = 0
        self._resolved_requests = 0
        self._batches = 0
        self._duplicated_slots = 0
        self._queue_wait_seconds = 0.0
        self._inference_busy_seconds = 0.0

    async def start(self) -> None:
        if self._runner is None:
            self._executor = ThreadPoolExecutor(
                max_workers=1,
                thread_name_prefix="neuron-embed",
            )
            self._runner = asyncio.create_task(self._run())

    async def stop(self) -> None:
        if self._runner is not None:
            self._runner.cancel()
            try:
                await self._runner
            except asyncio.CancelledError:
                pass
            self._runner = None
        if self._executor is not None:
            # The runner is cancelled only between batches or after the current
            # synchronous inference has completed. Shut down its dedicated
            # worker explicitly so FastAPI/uvicorn can exit without retaining
            # an asyncio default-executor thread.
            self._executor.shutdown(wait=True, cancel_futures=True)
            self._executor = None

    async def embed(self, texts: list[str]) -> list[list[float]]:
        if not texts:
            raise EmbeddingInputError("texts must not be empty")
        loop = asyncio.get_running_loop()
        futures = []
        enqueued_at = time.perf_counter()
        for text in texts:
            future = loop.create_future()
            futures.append(future)
            await self.queue.put(
                _WorkItem(text=text, future=future, enqueued_at=enqueued_at)
            )
        self._submitted_requests += len(futures)
        return await asyncio.gather(*futures)

    def metrics(self) -> dict[str, float | int]:
        """Return process-lifetime batcher timings, not device MFU."""
        uptime_seconds = time.perf_counter() - self._started_at
        busy_duty_percent = (
            100 * self._inference_busy_seconds / uptime_seconds
            if uptime_seconds > 0
            else 0.0
        )
        return {
            "uptime_seconds": uptime_seconds,
            "submitted_requests": self._submitted_requests,
            "resolved_requests": self._resolved_requests,
            "batches": self._batches,
            "duplicated_slots": self._duplicated_slots,
            "queue_wait_seconds": self._queue_wait_seconds,
            "inference_busy_seconds": self._inference_busy_seconds,
            "batcher_busy_duty_percent": busy_duty_percent,
            "queue_depth": self.queue.qsize(),
        }

    async def _run(self) -> None:
        while True:
            first = await self.queue.get()
            items = [first]
            deadline = asyncio.get_running_loop().time() + self.wait_seconds
            while len(items) < self.batch_size:
                remaining = deadline - asyncio.get_running_loop().time()
                if remaining <= 0:
                    break
                try:
                    items.append(await asyncio.wait_for(self.queue.get(), remaining))
                except asyncio.TimeoutError:
                    break

            batch_texts = [item.text for item in items]
            if len(batch_texts) == 1:
                batch_texts.append(batch_texts[0])
                self._duplicated_slots += 1

            inference_start = time.perf_counter()
            self._queue_wait_seconds += sum(
                inference_start - item.enqueued_at for item in items
            )
            try:
                executor = self._executor
                if executor is None:
                    raise RuntimeError("embedding batcher stopped while a batch was running")
                loop = asyncio.get_running_loop()
                embeddings = await loop.run_in_executor(
                    executor,
                    self.infer_batch,
                    batch_texts,
                )
                if len(embeddings) != self.batch_size:
                    raise RuntimeError(
                        f"embedding model returned {len(embeddings)} rows; "
                        f"expected {self.batch_size}"
                    )
                for item, embedding in zip(items, embeddings):
                        if not item.future.cancelled():
                            item.future.set_result(embedding)
                self._resolved_requests += len(items)
            except Exception as exc:
                for item in items:
                        if not item.future.cancelled():
                            item.future.set_exception(exc)
                self._resolved_requests += len(items)
            finally:
                self._inference_busy_seconds += time.perf_counter() - inference_start
                self._batches += 1
                for _ in items:
                    self.queue.task_done()


class NeuronEmbeddingModel:
    def __init__(
        self,
        compiled_model_path: str,
        tokenizer_path: str,
        sequence_length: int = 1024,
        embedding_dimension: int = 4096,
    ) -> None:
        from optimum.neuron import NeuronModelForEmbedding
        from transformers import AutoTokenizer

        self.sequence_length = sequence_length
        self.embedding_dimension = embedding_dimension
        self.tokenizer = AutoTokenizer.from_pretrained(
            tokenizer_path,
            padding_side="right",
        )
        self.model = NeuronModelForEmbedding.from_pretrained(compiled_model_path)

    def _validate_lengths(self, texts: list[str]) -> None:
        tokenized = self.tokenizer(
            texts,
            padding=False,
            truncation=False,
            add_special_tokens=True,
        )
        lengths = [len(input_ids) for input_ids in tokenized["input_ids"]]
        too_long = [length for length in lengths if length > self.sequence_length]
        if too_long:
            raise EmbeddingInputError(
                f"input has {max(too_long)} tokens; compiled limit is "
                f"{self.sequence_length}"
            )

    def embed_batch(self, texts: list[str]) -> list[list[float]]:
        if len(texts) != 2:
            raise ValueError("compiled embedding model requires exactly two inputs")
        self._validate_lengths(texts)
        tokens = self.tokenizer(
            texts,
            padding="max_length",
            truncation=False,
            max_length=self.sequence_length,
            return_tensors="pt",
        )
        outputs = self.model(**tokens)
        if hasattr(outputs, "last_hidden_state"):
            hidden_states = outputs.last_hidden_state
        elif isinstance(outputs, (tuple, list)):
            hidden_states = outputs[0]
        else:
            hidden_states = outputs

        if hidden_states.shape != (2, 1, self.embedding_dimension):
            raise RuntimeError(
                f"embedding model output shape is {tuple(hidden_states.shape)}; "
                f"expected (2, 1, {self.embedding_dimension})"
            )
        embeddings = hidden_states[:, 0, :]
        embeddings = F.normalize(embeddings.float(), p=2, dim=1)
        if embeddings.shape != (2, self.embedding_dimension):
            raise RuntimeError(
                f"embedding shape is {tuple(embeddings.shape)}; expected "
                f"(2, {self.embedding_dimension})"
            )
        return embeddings.cpu().tolist()


class EmbedRequest(BaseModel):
    texts: list[str] = Field(min_length=1, max_length=64)


class EmbedResponse(BaseModel):
    embeddings: list[list[float]]
    dimension: int


def create_app(model: NeuronEmbeddingModel, wait_ms: float = 5.0) -> FastAPI:
    batcher = StaticBatchEmbedder(model.embed_batch, wait_ms=wait_ms)

    @asynccontextmanager
    async def lifespan(_: FastAPI):
        await batcher.start()
        try:
            yield
        finally:
            await batcher.stop()

    app = FastAPI(lifespan=lifespan)

    @app.get("/health")
    async def health():
        return {
            "status": "ok",
            "batch_size": batcher.batch_size,
            "sequence_length": model.sequence_length,
            "dimension": model.embedding_dimension,
        }

    @app.get("/metrics")
    async def metrics():
        return {
            "batch_size": batcher.batch_size,
            "sequence_length": model.sequence_length,
            "dimension": model.embedding_dimension,
            **batcher.metrics(),
            "note": "Batcher busy duty is wall-clock inference occupancy, not Neuron MFU.",
        }

    @app.post("/embed", response_model=EmbedResponse)
    async def embed(request: EmbedRequest):
        try:
            embeddings = await batcher.embed(request.texts)
        except EmbeddingInputError as exc:
            raise HTTPException(status_code=422, detail=str(exc)) from exc
        except Exception as exc:
            raise HTTPException(status_code=500, detail=str(exc)) from exc
        return EmbedResponse(
            embeddings=embeddings,
            dimension=model.embedding_dimension,
        )

    return app


def main() -> None:
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="Serve Qwen3 embeddings on Neuron")
    parser.add_argument("--compiled-model-path", required=True)
    parser.add_argument("--tokenizer-path", required=True)
    parser.add_argument("--host", default="127.0.0.1")
    parser.add_argument("--port", type=int, default=6030)
    parser.add_argument("--sequence-length", type=int, default=1024)
    parser.add_argument("--embedding-dimension", type=int, default=4096)
    parser.add_argument("--microbatch-wait-ms", type=float, default=5.0)
    args = parser.parse_args()

    model = NeuronEmbeddingModel(
        compiled_model_path=args.compiled_model_path,
        tokenizer_path=args.tokenizer_path,
        sequence_length=args.sequence_length,
        embedding_dimension=args.embedding_dimension,
    )
    app = create_app(model, wait_ms=args.microbatch_wait_ms)

    import uvicorn

    uvicorn.run(app, host=args.host, port=args.port, workers=1)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

content.content:
[build-system]
requires = [
    "setuptools>=77.0.3,<80.0.0",
    "wheel"
]
build-backend = "setuptools.build_meta"

[project]
name = "vllm-neuron"
version = "0.21.0.1.0.0"
description = "vLLM Neuron backend plugin"
license = "Apache-2.0"
authors = [
    {name = "AWS Neuron team"}
]
classifiers = [
    "Programming Language :: Python :: 3.11",
    "Programming Language :: Python :: 3.12",
    "Programming Language :: Python :: 3.13",
]
requires-python = ">=3.11"
dynamic = ["dependencies", "optional-dependencies"]

[project.entry-points."vllm.platform_plugins"]
neuron = "vllm_neuron:register"

[tool.setuptools.dynamic]
dependencies = {file = ["requirements/core.txt"]}
optional-dependencies = {test = {file = ["requirements/test.txt"]}}

# Keep non-.py data files in the wheel (synthetic model JSONs loaded via
# Path(__file__).parent, Llama3 LICENSE, py.typed). Without this they get dropped
# and the synthetic model breaks at runtime.
[tool.setuptools]
include-package-data = true

[tool.setuptools.package-data]
vllm_neuron = ["py.typed", "**/*.json", "model/llama3/LICENSE"]

# Keep only the plugin package out of all the top-level dirs (test/, ci/, tools/,
# examples/, ...). The dotted form pins it to vllm_neuron and its subpackages, so
# a sibling like vllm_neuron_tests can't sneak in (a bare vllm_neuron* would).
[tool.setuptools.packages.find]
include = ["vllm_neuron", "vllm_neuron.*"]

[tool.pytest.ini_options]
testpaths = ["test/unit", "test/vllm_neuron"]
python_files = ["test_*.py", "*_test.py", "*_tests.py"]
python_classes = "Test*"
python_functions = "test_*"

[tool.coverage.run]
branch = true
source = ["vllm_neuron"]
omit = [
    "test/*",
    "ci/*",
    ".github/*",
    "doc/*",
    "*.md",
    "setup.py",
    "vllm_neuron/patches/*",
    "test/evaluation/*",
]

[tool.coverage.paths]
source = [
    "vllm_neuron/",
    "*/site-packages/",
]

[tool.coverage.report]
precision = 2
show_missing = true
skip_covered = false
exclude_lines = [
    "pragma: no cover",
    "def __repr__",
    "raise AssertionError",
    "raise NotImplementedError",
    "if __name__ == .__main__.:",
    "if TYPE_CHECKING:",
    "@abstractmethod",
]

[tool.ruff.lint.isort]
known-first-party = ["vllm_neuron"]

[tool.ruff.lint]
# G004: Prevent f-strings in logging statements
extend-select = ["G004"]

[tool.ruff.lint.per-file-ignores]
# Only enforce G004 (no f-strings in logging) in vllm_neuron folder
"test/*" = ["G004"]
"ci/*" = ["G004"]
"docs/*" = ["G004"]
"setup.py" = ["G004"]
# vLLM Neuron-specific ignores - intentional patterns in the codebase
# E741: Ambiguous variable names (I, l, E, H are standard ML dimension names)
# F841: Unused variables (often intentional for side effects in tests)
# E402: Module level import not at top (required for conditional imports)
# E701: Multiple statements on one line (NKI kernel patterns)
# E711/E712: Comparison to None/False (NKI kernel patterns)
# E721/E722: Type comparisons and bare except (legacy patterns)
"vllm_neuron/*" = ["E741", "F841", "E402", "E701", "E711", "E712", "E721", "E722", "G004"]
"test/vllm_neuron/*" = ["E741", "F841", "E402", "E701"]
"examples/vllm_neuron/*" = ["F841"]

[tool.mypy]
python_version = "3.11"
warn_return_any = true
warn_unused_configs = true
disallow_untyped_defs = false
check_untyped_defs = true
show_error_codes = true
show_column_numbers = true
show_error_context = true
pretty = true
ignore_missing_imports = true

[[tool.mypy.overrides]]
module = "tests.*"
ignore_errors = true

content.content:
# SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0
import os

from .llama3 import LlamaForCausalLM
from .gpt_oss import GptOssForCausalLM
from .llama3 import Eagle3LlamaForCausalLM
from .qwen3_vl import Qwen3VLForConditionalGeneration


def get_models() -> list[tuple[str, type]]:
    """Return a list of available model classes.

    Returns:
        list[tuple[str, type]]: A list of tuples containing model names and their corresponding classes.
            Each tuple contains (model_name, model_class) where:
            - model_name (str): The string identifier for the model, compatible with Hugging Face transformers architecture
            - model_class (type): The actual model class implementation
    """
    models = [
        ("LlamaForCausalLM", LlamaForCausalLM),
        ("GptOssForCausalLM", GptOssForCausalLM),
        ("Eagle3LlamaForCausalLM", Eagle3LlamaForCausalLM),
        ("Qwen3VLForConditionalGeneration", Qwen3VLForConditionalGeneration),
    ]

    # SyntheticNeuronModel is a testing-only model that replaces real neural
    # network computation with deterministic KV cache fill/verify. Useful for
    # validating infrastructure (KV transfer, sharding, block management)
    # without requiring model weights or compilation.
    # Not for production inference — gated to avoid exposing to customers.
    if os.environ.get("VLLM_NEURON_SYNTHETIC_MODEL") == "1":
        from .synthetic import SyntheticNeuronModel

        models.append(("SyntheticNeuronModel", SyntheticNeuronModel))

    return models

input.description: Check __init__.py of new plugin version

content.content:
# SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0
"""vLLM Neuron plugin module."""

import glob
import os
import sys
import warnings

os.environ["CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES"] = ""

from vllm_neuron import envs

# Redirect torch_neuronx → libtorch_neuronx_lite before anything imports it.
from vllm_neuron.utils.import_redirector import install as _install_redirector

if envs.VLLM_NEURON_LIBTORCH_NEURONX_LITE:
    _install_redirector()

# Initialize logging early so VLLM_NEURON_LOG_LEVEL takes effect
from vllm_neuron.logging_config import setup_logging as _setup_logging

_setup_logging()
import logging

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
# Enable prometheus multiprocess mode so that metrics observed in vLLM Neuron
# EngineCore/Worker processes (e.g. scheduler padding metrics) are written to
# shared mmap files and visible at the API server's /metrics endpoint.
# Note: Prometheus requires PROMETHEUS_MULTIPROC_DIR to be set before
# prometheus_client is imported anywhere. Typically a user would set the env
# var manually, but we set it during package init to set this up for the user.
# In case prometheus_client is imported before vLLM Neuron, users can
# set PROMETHEUS_MULTIPROC_DIR manually in their env to enable metrics.
if "PROMETHEUS_MULTIPROC_DIR" not in os.environ:
    import tempfile

    os.environ["PROMETHEUS_MULTIPROC_DIR"] = tempfile.mkdtemp(
        prefix="vllm_neuron_prometheus_"
    )

# Suppress PyTorch kernel override warnings
warnings.filterwarnings(
    "ignore",
    category=UserWarning,
    message=".*Overriding a previously registered kernel.*",
)
warnings.filterwarnings(
    "ignore", category=UserWarning, message=".*other operators may also be overridden.*"
)


def _is_neuron_dev() -> bool:
    """Detect Neuron device by checking for /dev/neuron* devices."""
    neuron_devices = glob.glob("/dev/neuron*")
    return len(neuron_devices) > 0


def _is_cpu_mode() -> bool:
    """Check if VLLM_NEURON_CPU_MODE is enabled via environment variable."""
    return os.environ.get("VLLM_NEURON_CPU_MODE", "0") == "1"


def _is_cpu_compile() -> bool:
    """Check if VLLM_NEURON_CPU_COMPILE is enabled via environment variable."""
    return os.environ.get("VLLM_NEURON_CPU_COMPILE", "0") == "1"


def _init_backend():
    """Initialize the vllm_neuron backend (XLA path)."""
    from vllm_neuron import envs

    if envs.is_native_backend():
        return

    # In CPU mode, the NKI CPU simulator is OFF by default.
    # Users opt-in by setting NKI_SIMULATOR=1 for kernel development,
    # integration and accuracy validation on small shapes and tiny models.
    if envs.VLLM_NEURON_CPU_MODE:
        if os.environ.get("NKI_SIMULATOR") == "1":
            os.environ.setdefault("NKI_PRECISE_FP", "1")

    if envs.VLLM_NEURON_CPU_MODE and _is_cpu_compile():
        raise RuntimeError(
            "VLLM_NEURON_CPU_MODE and VLLM_NEURON_CPU_COMPILE are not compatible with each other"
        )

    os.environ["PJRT_DEVICE"] = "CPU"

    import torch
    import torch._dynamo.backends.registry as registry

    _has_neuron_hw = _is_neuron_dev()

    torch_neuronx = None
    if not envs.VLLM_NEURON_CPU_MODE or _has_neuron_hw:
        try:
            import torch_neuronx  # noqa: E402
        except ImportError:
            pass

    from vllm_neuron.compile.backend import compile
    from vllm_neuron.compile.capture_backend import capture

    if "vllm_neuron" not in registry.list_backends():
        registry.register_backend(compiler_fn=compile, name="vllm_neuron")

    if "vllm_neuron_graph_capture" not in registry.list_backends():
        registry.register_backend(compiler_fn=capture, name="vllm_neuron_graph_capture")

    if torch_neuronx is not None:
        from vllm_neuron import overrides  # noqa: F401

        sys.modules["torch.neuron"] = torch_neuronx
        torch.neuron = torch_neuronx
        torch.neuron.is_available = lambda: True
        torch.neuron.current_device = lambda: 0

        if not envs.VLLM_NEURON_LIBTORCH_NEURONX_LITE:
            # libtorch_neuronx_lite does this internally
            torch.utils.rename_privateuse1_backend("neuron")
            unsupported_dtype = [
                torch.float64,
                torch.uint16,
                torch.uint32,
                torch.uint64,
            ]
            try:
                torch.utils.generate_methods_for_privateuse1_backend(
                    for_tensor=True,
                    for_module=True,
                    for_storage=True,
                    unsupported_dtype=unsupported_dtype,
                )
            except RuntimeError as e:
                if "already been registered" not in str(e):
                    raise
                logger.debug("privateuse1 backend already registered, skipping: %s", e)

    else:
        # CPU-only: register minimal privateuse1 backend so dynamo's
        # SymbolicStreamState doesn't fall through to CUDA
        import types

        torch.utils.rename_privateuse1_backend("neuron")
        neuron_module = types.ModuleType("torch.neuron")
        neuron_module.is_available = lambda: False
        neuron_module.current_device = lambda: 0
        neuron_module.current_stream = lambda device=None: type(
            "_S", (), {"device": torch.device("cpu")}
        )()
        neuron_module.set_stream = lambda stream: None
        sys.modules["torch.neuron"] = neuron_module
        torch.neuron = neuron_module

        try:
            torch.utils.generate_methods_for_privateuse1_backend(
                for_tensor=True,
                for_module=True,
                for_storage=True,
            )
        except RuntimeError:
            pass

    _original_current_accelerator = torch.accelerator.current_accelerator

    def _current_accelerator_wrapper(check_available: bool = False):
        device = _original_current_accelerator(check_available)
        if device is not None:
            if envs.VLLM_NEURON_CPU_MODE:
                return torch.device("cpu")
            elif envs.VLLM_NEURON_CPU_COMPILE:
                return torch.device("neuron", torch.neuron.current_device())
            elif device.type == "neuron" and device.index is None:
                return torch.device("neuron", torch.neuron.current_device())
            else:
                return device
        return device

    torch.accelerator.current_accelerator = _current_accelerator_wrapper

    # Patch accelerator stream/device APIs for CPU mode to prevent CUDA fallthrough
    if envs.VLLM_NEURON_CPU_MODE:
        torch.accelerator.current_stream = lambda device=None: type(
            "_S", (), {"device": torch.device("cpu")}
        )()
        torch.accelerator.current_device_index = lambda: 0


try:
    _init_backend()
except (ImportError, KeyError):
    pass

# Import-time so it survives spawn-mode re-imports (EngineCore subprocess
# never calls check_and_update_config).
from vllm_neuron.vllm.patches.port_hold_patch import apply_port_hold_patch

apply_port_hold_patch()


def register():
    """Register the Neuron platform if Neuron devices are present, else return None.

    The backend is selected based on the VLLM_NEURON_BACKEND environment variable:
    - "vllm_neuron": Use the vLLM Neuron backend (default)
    - "neuron_native": Use the neuron native backend

    If VLLM_NEURON_BACKEND is not set, defaults to vllm_neuron.
    """
    if not _is_cpu_mode() and not _is_cpu_compile() and not _is_neuron_dev():
        warnings.warn(
            "No Neuron devices found. Skipping Neuron plugin registration.",
            category=UserWarning,
        )
        return None

    from vllm_neuron.backend import get_platform_class
    from vllm_neuron.vllm.platform import _patch_dcp_config_validation

    _patch_dcp_config_validation()

    return get_platform_class()


def __getattr__(name):
    import importlib

    if name == "nn":
        return importlib.import_module(f".{name}", __name__)
    raise AttributeError(f"module {__name__!r} has no attribute {name!r}")

# SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0
from __future__ import annotations

from typing import Protocol, runtime_checkable

import torch
from transformers import PretrainedConfig
from vllm.multimodal.inputs import MultiModalFeatureSpec

from vllm_neuron.model.neuron_config import VisionNeuronConfig


@runtime_checkable
class SupportsVisionWarmup(Protocol):
    """Models that support vision encoder warmup implement this interface."""

    def build_vision_synthetic_inputs(
        self,
        bucket: int,
        vision_neuron_config: VisionNeuronConfig,
        device: torch.device,
    ) -> dict[str, torch.Tensor]:
        """Construct shape-only tensors matching the vision encoder forward signature."""
        ...


@runtime_checkable
class SupportsSpatialMerge(Protocol):
    """Models that perform spatial token merging in the vision encoder."""

    @classmethod
    def get_vision_token_merge_factor(cls, hf_config: PretrainedConfig) -> int:
        """Return how many raw vision tokens collapse into one embed token."""
        ...


@runtime_checkable
class SupportsMaxPixels(Protocol):
    """Models whose image processor supports a max_pixels resize cap.

    Models that honor it own the math that maps the pixel budget to a raw
    (pre-merge) vision-token count, since patch tiling differs across
    architectures.
    """

    @classmethod
    def get_max_pixels_token_count(
        cls, hf_config: PretrainedConfig, max_pixels: int
    ) -> int:

@runtime_checkable

    architectures.

input.description: Check neuron_config.py structure in new release

content.content:
# SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0
import math
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from typing import Any, List


# TODO: expand support of device sampling configs
class OnDeviceSamplingConfig:
    def __init__(self, **kwargs: Any):
        all_greedy_val = kwargs.pop("all_greedy", False)
        # Handle string "true"/"false" from config
        if isinstance(all_greedy_val, str):
            self.all_greedy = all_greedy_val.lower() in ("true", "1", "yes")
        else:
            self.all_greedy = bool(all_greedy_val)

        self.max_top_k = int(kwargs.pop("max_top_k", 256))

        deterministic_val = kwargs.pop("deterministic", False)
        if isinstance(deterministic_val, str):
            self.deterministic = deterministic_val.lower() in ("true", "1", "yes")
        else:
            self.deterministic = bool(deterministic_val)

        # Data-parallel sampling degree (1 = disabled, >1 = batch sharding)
        self.sampling_dp_degree = int(kwargs.pop("sampling_dp_degree", 1))

        # Debug/validation only: when True, capture the sampling top-k outputs
        # (values, indices) via the tensor-capture facility. No-op unless a
        # tensor_capture config is also active (TensorRegistry must be set); the
        # if-guard folds to a constant at trace time, so the default-False path
        # leaves the compiled graph unchanged.
        capture_topk_val = kwargs.pop("capture_topk", False)
        if isinstance(capture_topk_val, str):
            self.capture_topk = capture_topk_val.lower() in ("true", "1", "yes")
        else:
            self.capture_topk = bool(capture_topk_val)


@dataclass
class TensorCaptureConfig:
    """Configuration for tensor capture debugging.

    Args:
        modules: List of module paths for hook-based capture
            (e.g., ["model.layers.0", "lm_head"])
        capture_dir: Directory to save captured tensors
        capture_filter: Optional list of tensor names for write-time
            filtering. When set, only captured tensors whose names match
            this list are written to disk; all others are silently
            skipped.
    """

    modules: List[str] = field(default_factory=list)
    capture_dir: str = "/tmp/vllm_neuron_tensor_captures"
    capture_filter: List[str] | None = None


@dataclass
class TensorReplacementConfig:
    """Configuration for tensor replacement.

    Args:
        tensors: Per-prompt reference tensors as a list of
            {module_name: [step_tensors]} dicts, where list index
            is the prompt index, step 0 is prefill, steps 1+ are decode.
        tensors_path: Path to a torch.save'd file containing
            tensors. Used for online serving where tensors cannot
            be passed in-memory via JSON config.
        prompt_token_ids: Per-prompt input token IDs used to match
            incoming requests to the correct prompt captures via
            prefix hashing. Required for multi-prompt validation.
        prompt_token_ids_path: Path to a torch.save'd file containing
            prompt_token_ids. Used for online serving.
    """

    tensors: list = field(default_factory=list, repr=False)
    tensors_path: str | None = None
    prompt_token_ids: list | None = field(default=None, repr=False)
    prompt_token_ids_path: str | None = None

    def resolve(self) -> tuple[list, list | None]:
        """Validate config and load tensors/prompt_token_ids from paths if needed."""
        import torch

        tensors = self.tensors
        if tensors and self.tensors_path:
            raise ValueError(
                "tensor_replacement: specify either 'tensors' or "
                "'tensors_path', not both."
            )
        if not tensors and self.tensors_path:
            tensors = torch.load(self.tensors_path, weights_only=False)
        if not tensors:
            raise ValueError(
                "tensor_replacement: must specify either 'tensors' or "
                "'tensors_path' with valid data."
            )

        prompt_token_ids = self.prompt_token_ids
        if not prompt_token_ids and self.prompt_token_ids_path:
            prompt_token_ids = torch.load(
                self.prompt_token_ids_path, weights_only=False
            )

        return tensors, prompt_token_ids

    def __repr__(self):
        if self.tensors_path:
            return f"TensorReplacementConfig(path={self.tensors_path!r})"
        n_prompts = len(self.tensors)
        n_modules = len(self.tensors[0]) if n_prompts > 0 else 0
        return f"TensorReplacementConfig(prompts={n_prompts}, modules={n_modules})"


@dataclass
class NeuronConfig:
    # TODO: Revisit sharding config consistency between vLLM Neuron and vLLM.
    # In the case of EP, we define an ep_degree argument in NeuronConfig because vLLM doesn't
    # support configuring EP degree.

# SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0
"""
Neuron Platform implementation for vLLM

This module provides the NeuronPlatform class that integrates vLLM Neuron with vLLM's
platform plug-in system.
"""

import logging
import os
import sys
import time as _time
from multiprocessing.process import BaseProcess
from typing import TYPE_CHECKING

import torch
from vllm_neuron import envs

from vllm.platforms import Platform, PlatformEnum
from vllm.v1.attention.backends.registry import AttentionBackendEnum

if TYPE_CHECKING:
    from vllm.attention.selector import AttentionSelectorConfig
    from vllm.config import VllmConfig
    from vllm.inputs import EngineInput
    from vllm.sampling_params import SamplingParams
    from vllm.pooling_params import PoolingParams
else:
    VllmConfig = None

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)

SO_DISABLED_MESSAGE = (
    "Structured outputs were requested, but this Neuron server was started "
    "with additional_config.neuron_config.enable_structured_outputs=false. "
    "Restart the server with enable_structured_outputs=true to serve "
    "structured-output requests. For this SO-off-only server, retry the "
    "request without structured_outputs."
)


# Relax DCP config validation for prefill-side DCP where TP <= num_kv_heads.
# Upstream asserts TP > num_kv_heads when DCP is enabled, but prefill DCP
# with apply_prefill_dcp=True shards attention across the DCP sub-group, not full TP.
#
# During plugin registration, vllm.config.model may not be importable due to
# circular imports. We attempt eagerly; if that fails we use sys.addaudithook
# to patch as soon as the module loads. The hook self-disables after one
# successful patch, so ongoing overhead is a single boolean check per import.
_dcp_config_patched = False


def _patch_dcp_config_validation():
    """Patch ModelConfig.verify_with_parallel_config to skip DCP checks on prefill.

    Called during plugin registration. If the import fails (circular import),
    installs an audit hook that patches on first successful import of
    vllm.config.model. The hook is lightweight (one boolean check) after
    firing and cannot be removed per CPython API, but the cost is negligible
    compared to import overhead itself.
    """
    global _dcp_config_patched
    if _dcp_config_patched:
        return
    _dcp_config_patched = True

    try:
        from vllm.config.model import ModelConfig

        _apply_dcp_patch(ModelConfig)
    except (ImportError, AttributeError):
        # Circular import during plugin registration. Use an audit hook to
        # apply the patch as soon as vllm.config.model finishes loading.
        # The hook fires on every "import" event but self-disables (via
        # _applied flag) after the first successful patch, reducing ongoing
        # cost to a single boolean check per subsequent import.
        import sys

        _applied = [False]

        def _audit_hook(event, args):
            if _applied[0]:
                return
            if event == "import" and "vllm.config" in str(args[0]):
                mod = sys.modules.get("vllm.config.model")
                if mod is not None and hasattr(mod, "ModelConfig"):
                    _applied[0] = True
                    _apply_dcp_patch(mod.ModelConfig)

        sys.addaudithook(_audit_hook)


def _apply_dcp_patch(ModelConfig):
    """Monkey-patch verify_with_parallel_config to bypass DCP assertion for prefill."""
    if getattr(
        ModelConfig.__dict__.get("verify_with_parallel_config"),
        "_neuron_dcp_patched",
        False,
    ):
        return

# SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0
"""
Llama Config
======================

<-- MODEL-SPECIFIC: All fields in this config are Llama-specific.
When porting to a new model, update all fields to match the target architecture.
"""

import json
from dataclasses import dataclass, field

import torch
from transformers import PretrainedConfig

from vllm_neuron.model.neuron_config import NeuronConfig

from .quantization import QuantizationSpec


@dataclass
class LlamaConfig:
    # <-- MODEL-SPECIFIC: Architecture parameters
    vocab_size: int = 128256
    draft_vocab_size: int | None = None
    norm_before_residual: bool = False
    hidden_size: int = 2048
    unpadded_hidden_size: int | None = None
    intermediate_size: int = 8192
    num_hidden_layers: int = 16
    num_attention_heads: int = 32
    num_key_value_heads: int = 8
    head_dim: int = 64
    max_position_embeddings: int = 131072
    rms_norm_eps: float = 1e-5
    rope_parameters: dict = field(
        default_factory=lambda: {"rope_type": "default", "rope_theta": 500000.0}
    )
    tie_word_embeddings: bool = True
    torch_dtype: torch.dtype = torch.bfloat16

    # Framework config
    neuron_config: NeuronConfig | None = None

    # Quantization spec parsed from the HuggingFace ``quantization_config``
    # (populated by :meth:`from_configs`). ``None`` means "not quantized".
    # Modeling code should query this via
    # ``quant_spec.get_scheme_for_module(prefix)`` to decide weight dtypes
    # and kernel calls on a per-module basis.
    quant_spec: QuantizationSpec | None = field(default=None)

    def __post_init__(self):
        if self.head_dim is None:
            self.head_dim = self.hidden_size // self.num_attention_heads
        if self.unpadded_hidden_size is None:
            self.unpadded_hidden_size = self.hidden_size

    @classmethod
    def from_configs(
        cls, hf_config: PretrainedConfig, neuron_config: NeuronConfig = None
    ):
        if isinstance(hf_config, (str, bytes)):
            with open(hf_config) as f:
                config_dict = json.load(f)
        elif isinstance(hf_config, PretrainedConfig):
            config_dict = hf_config.to_dict()
            if hasattr(hf_config, "torch_dtype") and hf_config.torch_dtype is not None:
                config_dict["torch_dtype"] = hf_config.torch_dtype
        else:
            config_dict = hf_config

        field_names = {f.name for f in cls.__dataclass_fields__.values()}
        filtered_dict = {k: v for k, v in config_dict.items() if k in field_names}

        # Handle RedHat Eagle3 nested config: model params are inside
        # transformer_layer_config, with draft_vocab_size at the top level
        if (
            "transformer_layer_config" in config_dict
            and "hidden_size" not in filtered_dict
        ):
